behavior_summary: MOVE::0080 is a repeating NPC/item AI/process attached to an entity (entity pointer passed as parameter). It periodically inspects that entity's properties, updates per-entity info slots, optionally greets the player, computes facing, and issues pathfinding/movement orders.

The script queries multiple entity attributes (via FREE::1E6A and FREE::1D62) and uses their values plus the global flag arcadionMet to choose between a short-range interaction (greet + short pathfind) and an alternate movement behavior (longer range pathfind).

When the greeting/interaction branch is taken the script: computes direction to a target (Item::getDirToItem), spawns a direction-apply helper (METHOD::091C), makes the entity bark a fixed string ('Greetings Disciple.'), and calls Npc::pathfind with a close-range parameter (0x04).

The process also spawns short-lived helpers/timers (METHOD::0AC6 with a retval from FREE::1A96, and FREE::1C19 with pid and a small numeric) to schedule follow-up/timed behavior, then suspends and repeats.
notable_control_flow: Entry is a periodic/suspended process (execution_form: process) that repeatedly suspends and resumes rather than returning once.
Early in the script two per-entity info slots (IDs like 0x0205 and 0x0204) are set (via FREE::2A56/set info) to prepare state before behavior decisions.
A numeric property from FREE::1E6A is compared against 0x1E and another from FREE::1D62 against 0x40; only when those checks allow does the greeting/pathfind behavior execute.
A guard using the global flag arcadionMet changes the pathfinding parameter (close 0x04 vs longer 0x28), altering movement distance/behavior.
Several actions are performed by spawning helper methods (METHOD::091C, METHOD::0AC6, FREE::1C19) rather than inline, so timing and sequencing are delegated to those spawned processes.
open_questions: Exact semantic meanings of FREE::1E6A and FREE::1D62 returns (which concrete entity fields they read) remain uncertain — they appear to be small numeric state or type fields.
Precise semantics of entity info slots 0x0205 and 0x0204 (what each slot represents) are inferred only from their use as setup flags.
Exact trigger/activation conditions that start MOVE::0080 (area entry vs. NPC start vs. always-running update) are inferred from suspend/resume behavior but not explicit in the excerpt.
What the small numeric arguments to FREE::1C19 and METHOD::0AC6 represent (timing, priority, or animation indices) is likely but not certain.

main_function.name: U8P_MOVE::0080
main_function.purpose: Periodic AI/update routine for an NPC/entity that inspects the entity, sets per-entity info, optionally greets the player, adjusts facing, and issues pathfinding commands while scheduling short helper tasks.
entry_conditions: Attached to an entity and runs as a suspended/resumed process (periodic update loop).
Behaviors branch based on entity properties returned by FREE::1E6A and FREE::1D62 and the global arcadionMet flag.

function_map.name: U8P_MOVE::0080
function_map.role: NPC/item periodic AI/update process that inspects an entity and issues movement/speech/timers.

function_map.name: METHOD::091C
function_map.role: Spawned helper that applies a computed facing/direction or starts a direction-dependent action on the entity.

function_map.name: METHOD::0AC6
function_map.role: Spawned timed/delayed helper; accepts a small numeric parameter (retval from FREE::1A96) and the entity pointer to schedule a short action.

function_map.name: FREE::1C19
function_map.role: Spawned timer/event helper taking pid and a small numeric to schedule a short follow-up task.

function_map.name: Item::getDirToItem
function_map.role: Compute direction from actor/pid to target entity/item; result used to spawn METHOD::091C.

function_map.name: process Item::bark
function_map.role: Make the entity speak/display a short string (used for the greeting 'Greetings Disciple.').

function_map.name: process Npc::pathfind
function_map.role: Issue pathfinding/movement orders toward a specified target/index with a movement/range parameter (values observed: 0x04 and 0x28).

function_map.name: FREE::1E6A
function_map.role: Query a numeric property of the entity (called with a small flag 0x01); return is compared to constants (e.g., 0x1E).

function_map.name: FREE::1D62
function_map.role: Query another numeric attribute of the entity; return compared against 0x40 to gate behavior.

function_map.name: FREE::2A56
function_map.role: Set/modify entity info slots (called with slot ids like 0x0205 and 0x0204 and the entity pointer).

function_map.name: FREE::1A96
function_map.role: Return a small numeric used as a parameter for METHOD::0AC6 (likely a random/timer value).

internal_calls.purpose: The script repeatedly suspends and effectively re-invokes/continues itself as a long-running process (periodic update).

external_calls.purpose: Set per-entity info slots (IDs like 0x0205 and 0x0204) for state preparation before behavior decisions.
Read a numeric entity property (used to compare against 0x1E to choose the interaction branch).
Read an additional numeric attribute of the entity (checked against 0x40) to gate greeting/pathfinding.
Compute direction from pid/actor to the target to allow the entity to face the player before greeting or moving.
Spawn a helper to apply the computed facing/direction to the entity (uses retval from Item::getDirToItem).
Make the entity speak the fixed greeting message 'Greetings Disciple.' in the interaction branch.
Command NPC movement toward the chosen target/index ([BP-06h]) with different range parameters (0x04 for close/greeting, 0x28 for alternate branch when arcadionMet is set).
Obtain a small numeric (likely a delay or configuration value) which is then passed to METHOD::0AC6 for a timed helper spawn.
Spawn a timed/delayed helper using the numeric returned by FREE::1A96 and the entity pointer.
Spawn a small timer/event helper with pid and a small numeric (0 or 0x1E depending on branch) to schedule follow-up behavior.

reads.name: arcadionMet
reads.likely_effect: Alter choice of pathfinding/movement parameter (choose close-range interaction vs longer-range movement).

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: MOVE::0080 is a repeating NPC/item AI/process attached to an entity (entity pointer passed as parameter). It periodically inspects that entity's properties, updates per-entity info slots, optionally greets the player, computes facing, and issues pathfinding/movement orders.
